title: WebinarTV Secretly Scraped Zoom Meetings of Anonymous Recovery Programs
summary: WebinarTV scraped and shared 12 steps-based anonymous meetings for people recovering from addiction and other private support groups.
reason: This is a concrete case of enclosure: the common substrate of mutual care and peer support being extracted, repackaged, and routed through a commercial platform without consent, with particular harm to vulnerable populations whose safety depends on the non-marketized nature of that space.
angle: Use the 'commune layer' tool — these recovery meetings are a near-pure example of care routed through people rather than market or state intermediaries, and WebinarTV's scraping is a textbook capture operation targeting precisely the trust-based, off-market quality that makes the commons functional.

title: Virginia Public Workers Make Headway on Bargaining Rights
summary: After a years-long campaign by unions, Virginia’s General Assembly passed legislation to extend collective bargaining rights to nearly half a million state, county, and municipal government employees. Union recognition has been denied Virginia’s public employees since 1946 when the state legislature passed a joint resolution against public sector bargaining to defeat a Black hospital workers’ organizing drive at the University of Virginia. A 1977 state Supreme Court ruling affirmed the ban, which was later codified by legislation in 1993.

title: London has fallen to crime and feral youth? Rubbish | Letters

But the deeper issue is not only exaggeration. It is the ease with which young people, once visible in public space, are turned into signs of disorder before they have done anything at all. Society does not merely fear what some young people do; it fears their collective presence. Teenagers gathering on a high street are too quickly read as menace, excess or incipient criminality.
